About सोऽहम्

"I am That" — Isha Upanishad

"The Knower of Brahman becomes Brahman."
— Mundaka Upanishad 3.2.9

Azure DevOps Engineer at IBM, Bengaluru — building cloud infrastructure that is resilient, automated, and self-healing. Computer Science Engineer with a 9.56 CGPA and a parallel obsession: the Upanishads, Advaita Vedanta, and the question that never resolves —
Who is the one deploying?

AZ-104 Certified AWS CLF-C02 GFG Rank #206 CGPA 9.56 IBM · Bengaluru Cloud Vedantin

Projects सृष्टि

"यत् भावयति तत् भवति" — What one envisions, one becomes

01

Banking System

धर्म · Dharma

Secure transaction management with SQL backend integrity — a system built on trust, precision, and immutable records. Like karma itself: every action recorded, nothing lost.

C++SQL OOPBackend
02

E-Healthcare Advisor

करुणा · Karuṇā

ML-based symptom analysis for disease prediction and intelligent doctor recommendations. Compassion encoded in mathematics — healing through the lens of data.

PythonML FlaskHealthcare
03

Sorting Visualizer

क्रम · Krama

Visual implementation of Bubble, Quick, and Merge Sort algorithms. Making the invisible patterns of cosmic order visible — because the universe itself sorts by dharma.

JavaScriptAlgorithms VisualizationDSA

Experience अनुभव

"Direct experience — Not what is taught, but what is lived" — Vedantic principle

Feb 2025 — Present

Azure DevOps Engineer

IBM · Bengaluru, India

  • Hardened cloud security via Azure PIM and RBAC — Just-In-Time access enforcement
  • Achieved 99.5% patching compliance using Azure Automation Runbooks
  • Maintained 99.9% system uptime through proactive monitoring pipelines
  • Reduced manual overhead by ~30% via scripting automation
  • Identified idle assets → 15% reduction in monthly cloud costs
2020 — 2024

B.Tech. Computer Science Engineering

Institute of Engineering and Management, Kolkata

  • Forged in the disciplines of IoT, Cloud Computing, and Smart Business Technology — where silicon meets strategy
  • CGPA 9.56 — not a number, a direction of will
  • Spent four years asking the question every engineer must face — not just how systems work, but why they exist at all
  • Foundations laid in Data Structures, DBMS, and System Design — the grammar of the digital universe

Philosophy दर्शन

"Vision" — Seeing truth beyond Maya

एकमेवाद्वितीयम्

Ekam evādvitīyam

"There is only One — without a second."

— Chāndogya Upaniṣad 6.2.1

अद्वैत

Advaita

Non-Duality in Code

There is no separation between the system and its observer. The engineer IS the architecture. Subject and object dissolve in deep flow state.

Engineering Vedanta

साक्षी

Sākṣī

The Observer Effect

Monitoring changes the system. True reliability comes from witnessing without attachment — alert but undisturbed, like consciousness itself.

Cloud Awareness

निर्गुण

Nirguṇa

Stateless Architecture

Like Brahman without attributes — pure, without state, infinitely scalable. The best systems hold no memory of what they have processed.

Architecture Design

निष्काम कर्म

Niṣkāma Karma

Egoless Deployment

Action without attachment to outcome. Deploy, observe, release — the Gita's teaching applied to every pipeline. The work is the offering.

DevOps Gita

माया

Māyā

Complexity is Illusion

Every complex system is built from simple, repeating patterns. Strip away the layers — at the core of every architecture is elegant simplicity.

Systems Clarity

ऋत

Ṛta

The Cosmic Order

Ṛta — the Vedic principle of cosmic order — is what we call best practices. Pipelines that follow natural patterns rarely break. Order is not imposed, it is discovered.

DevOps Vedic
"I approach engineering the way Advaita Vedanta approaches consciousness — every system, every architecture, every line of code is the same universal intelligence expressing itself through a different form."
— Amarjeet Anand

"The dialogue between two — where the separate discover they are One" — Bhagavad Gita

"असतो मा सद्गमय"

Asato mā sad gamaya

"From the unreal, lead me to the real"

— Bṛhadāraṇyaka Upaniṣad 1.3.28
